capabilities.name: Identity / SSO
capabilities.catalog_intro: The identity perimeter. Everything downstream — SaaS access, device trust, privileged escalation — depends on getting this layer right.
capabilities.description: Authentication, SSO, directory

sub_capabilities.name: Single Sign-On
sub_capabilities.rationale: One credential set unlocks all federated apps, reducing password reuse and shadow IT.

sub_capabilities.name: MFA / Passwordless
sub_capabilities.rationale: MFA blocks the majority of credential-based attacks; passwordless eliminates the phishable password entirely.

sub_capabilities.name: Directory / SCIM
sub_capabilities.rationale: Centralised directory with SCIM provisioning ensures accounts are created and removed consistently across all integrated systems.

sub_capabilities.name: Lifecycle Management
sub_capabilities.rationale: Automated joiner/mover/leaver workflows prevent stale accounts that provide persistent attacker footholds.

sub_capabilities.name: Adaptive / Risk Auth
sub_capabilities.rationale: Contextual risk signals (location, device, behaviour) step up authentication dynamically without degrading user experience.

capabilities.name: Endpoint Protection
capabilities.catalog_intro: Your largest attack surface. Endpoints are the entry point for 70%+ of breaches; detection and prevention quality here directly determines dwell time.
capabilities.description: EDR/EPP for workstations and servers

sub_capabilities.name: EDR / Threat Detection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Behavioural telemetry and detection rules identify attackers post-compromise before they can move laterally or exfiltrate.

sub_capabilities.name: Prevention / EPP
sub_capabilities.rationale: Signature and ML-based prevention stops known malware and commodity threats before execution, reducing analyst noise.

sub_capabilities.name: Threat Intelligence
sub_capabilities.rationale: IOC and TTP feeds enriched against endpoint telemetry accelerate alert triage and proactive blocking.

sub_capabilities.name: Device Control
sub_capabilities.rationale: USB and removable media controls prevent data exfiltration and introduction of malicious media.

sub_capabilities.name: Server / Linux Protection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Servers and Linux workloads are high-value targets; EDR coverage must extend beyond Windows desktops.

sub_capabilities.name: Automated Response
sub_capabilities.rationale: Automated isolation and remediation actions reduce mean time to contain, limiting blast radius during an active incident.

capabilities.name: Email Security
capabilities.catalog_intro: The primary delivery vector for phishing, BEC, and ransomware. A first line of defence that must handle volume and sophistication simultaneously.
capabilities.description: Phishing, BEC, malware filtering

sub_capabilities.name: Anti-Phishing / BEC
sub_capabilities.rationale: Impersonation detection and BEC pattern analysis stop the most costly attack vector before messages reach the inbox.

sub_capabilities.name: Malware / Attachment Sandboxing
sub_capabilities.rationale: Dynamic detonation of attachments and links in an isolated environment catches zero-day payloads that signature scanning misses.

sub_capabilities.name: DMARC / SPF / DKIM Enforcement
sub_capabilities.rationale: Enforcing sender authentication standards prevents domain spoofing and reduces spoofed phishing delivered under your brand.

sub_capabilities.name: URL Rewriting / Click-Time Protection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Rewriting links and rechecking them at click time catches late-stage phishing redirects that bypass initial scanning.

sub_capabilities.name: Outbound DLP / Data Protection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Outbound content inspection prevents sensitive data from leaving via email, covering both accidental leakage and insider threat.

capabilities.name: Backup / DR
capabilities.catalog_intro: Your last resort when everything else fails. Backup integrity and recovery speed determine whether a ransomware event becomes a business continuity crisis.
capabilities.description: Data backup, recovery, ransomware resilience

sub_capabilities.name: Recovery Testing / RTO Validation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Regular restore drills confirm backups are actually valid and that RTO targets are achievable under real incident conditions.

sub_capabilities.name: Immutable / Air-Gapped Storage
sub_capabilities.rationale: Backup copies that cannot be encrypted or deleted by ransomware are the only reliable guarantee of recovery after an attack.

sub_capabilities.name: Ransomware Detection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Anomaly detection on backup jobs flags unusual encryption activity early, potentially catching ransomware before full propagation.

sub_capabilities.name: Cloud / SaaS Backup
sub_capabilities.rationale: Cloud-hosted data in M365, Google Workspace, and Salesforce is not automatically protected by the provider; explicit backup coverage is required.

sub_capabilities.name: Granular Restore
sub_capabilities.rationale: Item-level restore for files, emails, and databases reduces recovery time and avoids the disruption of full system restoration.

capabilities.name: MDM / UEM
capabilities.catalog_intro: Policy enforcement for the device fleet. Without MDM, you cannot verify device posture before granting access — the foundation of zero-trust on endpoints.
capabilities.description: Mobile/device management and policy enforcement

sub_capabilities.name: Device Enrollment / MDM Profile
sub_capabilities.rationale: Managed device enrollment ensures only known, policy-compliant devices can access corporate resources.

sub_capabilities.name: Compliance Policy Enforcement
sub_capabilities.rationale: Enforcing encryption, OS patch level, and screen lock requirements on devices is the baseline for conditional access decisions.

sub_capabilities.name: Remote Wipe / Lock
sub_capabilities.rationale: Lost or stolen devices can be immediately locked or wiped to prevent data exposure without relying on the device holder.

sub_capabilities.name: App Management / MAM
sub_capabilities.rationale: Mobile application management controls which apps are permitted on devices and enforces data separation on BYOD endpoints.

sub_capabilities.name: Certificate / Wi-Fi / VPN Provisioning
sub_capabilities.rationale: Automated certificate and network profile provisioning removes friction from secure connectivity while preventing rogue device access.

capabilities.name: Password Manager
capabilities.catalog_intro: Credential hygiene at scale. Reduces password reuse, enables secure sharing, and provides an audit trail for credential access.
capabilities.description: Enterprise credential vault and sharing

sub_capabilities.name: Encrypted Vault Storage
sub_capabilities.rationale: End-to-end encrypted credential storage eliminates password reuse and enables strong unique passwords across all accounts.

sub_capabilities.name: Secure Credential Sharing
sub_capabilities.rationale: Controlled credential sharing prevents credentials from being transmitted over insecure channels like email or chat.

sub_capabilities.name: Breach / Dark Web Monitoring
sub_capabilities.rationale: Alerting on compromised credentials identified in data breaches enables rapid credential rotation before accounts are exploited.

sub_capabilities.name: SSO / SAML Integration
sub_capabilities.rationale: Integration with IdP enables centralised authentication to the vault and enforces MFA without requiring separate vault credentials.

sub_capabilities.name: Admin Audit Logs
sub_capabilities.rationale: Audit logs of credential access and sharing events support forensic investigation and compliance reporting.

capabilities.name: Cloud Security
capabilities.catalog_intro: Visibility and control across cloud workloads. Cloud misconfigurations are the leading cause of cloud breaches; posture management closes that gap.
capabilities.description: Cloud workload protection, IaaS/SaaS security posture

sub_capabilities.name: CSPM / Posture Management
sub_capabilities.rationale: Continuous detection of cloud misconfigurations across IaaS closes the gap that accounts for the majority of cloud-origin breaches.

sub_capabilities.name: Cloud Workload Protection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Runtime protection for VMs, containers, and serverless workloads detects threats that network-level controls cannot see.

sub_capabilities.name: Cloud Identity / CIEM
sub_capabilities.rationale: Cloud infrastructure entitlement management identifies over-privileged IAM roles and unused permissions that expand the blast radius of compromise.

sub_capabilities.name: Cloud Network Monitoring / VPC Flow
sub_capabilities.rationale: VPC flow log analysis and DNS monitoring detect lateral movement and data exfiltration within cloud environments.

sub_capabilities.name: Cloud Threat Detection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Correlating cloud service API logs, identity events, and workload telemetry surfaces attacker behaviour invisible to traditional SIEM.

capabilities.name: Vulnerability Management
capabilities.catalog_intro: The risk prioritisation engine. Knowing what's exposed — and fixing the right things first — separates proactive from reactive security programs.
capabilities.description: Asset scanning, risk prioritisation, patch tracking

sub_capabilities.name: Asset Discovery / Inventory
sub_capabilities.rationale: You cannot protect what you cannot see; authenticated and unauthenticated scanning builds the asset inventory that drives all downstream prioritisation.

sub_capabilities.name: Risk Prioritisation / CVSS Scoring
sub_capabilities.rationale: CVSS and exploit-probability scoring focus finite remediation resources on the vulnerabilities most likely to be weaponised.

sub_capabilities.name: Patch Tracking / Remediation Workflow
sub_capabilities.rationale: Ticketing integration and SLA tracking ensure identified vulnerabilities are remediated within policy timelines and exceptions are documented.

sub_capabilities.name: External Attack Surface Monitoring
sub_capabilities.rationale: Continuous monitoring of internet-facing assets from an attacker perspective identifies shadow IT and newly exposed services before they are exploited.

sub_capabilities.name: Compliance / Benchmark Scanning
sub_capabilities.rationale: CIS Benchmark and DISA STIG scanning provides evidence of hardening posture required for regulatory audits and insurance questionnaires.

capabilities.name: SIEM / MDR
capabilities.catalog_intro: The detection and response nerve centre. Log everything, correlate what matters, escalate what's real, and respond before dwell time compounds.
capabilities.description: Threat detection, alert triage, managed response

sub_capabilities.name: Log Ingestion / SIEM
sub_capabilities.rationale: Centralised log aggregation from endpoints, network, identity, and cloud is the prerequisite for any detection and investigation capability.

sub_capabilities.name: Detection & Correlation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Cross-source correlation rules and ML-based anomaly detection identify attacker behaviours that individual source logs cannot reveal alone.

sub_capabilities.name: SOAR / Automation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Playbook-driven automation for common alert types reduces analyst toil and mean time to respond for high-volume, low-complexity detections.

sub_capabilities.name: Managed Detection (MDR)
sub_capabilities.rationale: 24/7 expert analyst coverage fills the gap for organisations that cannot maintain a mature in-house SOC capability.

sub_capabilities.name: Threat Hunting
sub_capabilities.rationale: Proactive hypothesis-driven hunting surfaces attacker dwell that automated detections have not yet triggered on.

sub_capabilities.name: Compliance Reporting
sub_capabilities.rationale: Pre-built compliance dashboards and report exports reduce the manual effort required for SOC 2, PCI, and HIPAA audit evidence packages.

capabilities.name: Network Firewall / NGFW
capabilities.catalog_intro: The perimeter and internal traffic control plane. Modern NGFWs combine stateful inspection, IPS, and application-layer visibility in one control point.
capabilities.description: Perimeter defence, IPS/IDS, traffic inspection

sub_capabilities.name: NGFW / Stateful Inspection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Stateful packet inspection with application awareness is the baseline control for all inbound and outbound traffic at the perimeter.

sub_capabilities.name: IPS / IDS
sub_capabilities.rationale: Inline intrusion prevention signatures and anomaly detection block known exploits and flag suspicious traffic patterns at wire speed.

sub_capabilities.name: URL Filtering
sub_capabilities.rationale: Category-based and reputation-based URL filtering blocks malicious and policy-violating destinations before connection is established.

sub_capabilities.name: SSL Inspection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Decrypting TLS traffic for inspection prevents attackers from hiding command-and-control and exfiltration in encrypted channels.

sub_capabilities.name: Microsegmentation
sub_capabilities.rationale: East-west traffic controls within the network limit lateral movement after an initial compromise, containing the blast radius.

capabilities.name: CNAPP
capabilities.catalog_intro: Cloud-native application protection from code to cloud. Unifies CSPM, CWPP, and supply chain security for organisations running containerised or serverless workloads.
capabilities.description: Cloud-native app protection — workload, container, pipeline, CSPM

sub_capabilities.name: CSPM
sub_capabilities.rationale: Cloud security posture management detects misconfigurations across multi-cloud environments continuously, not just at point-in-time audit.

sub_capabilities.name: CWPP / Container Security
sub_capabilities.rationale: Runtime protection for containers and Kubernetes workloads detects abnormal process behaviour and prevents privilege escalation in cloud-native environments.

sub_capabilities.name: Supply Chain / Pipeline Security
sub_capabilities.rationale: Scanning container images, IaC templates, and third-party dependencies in the CI pipeline prevents vulnerabilities from reaching production.

sub_capabilities.name: API Security Posture
sub_capabilities.rationale: Discovering and assessing API endpoints for authentication gaps and data exposure is increasingly critical as APIs become the primary integration surface.

sub_capabilities.name: Secrets Detection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Detecting hardcoded credentials, API keys, and certificates in repositories and container images prevents trivial cloud account compromise.

capabilities.name: PAM
capabilities.catalog_intro: Privileged access is the crown jewels of your identity estate. Controlling, recording, and time-limiting privileged sessions is non-negotiable at enterprise scale.
capabilities.description: Privileged access management, session recording, just-in-time

sub_capabilities.name: Privileged Credential Vault
sub_capabilities.rationale: Vaulting privileged credentials with automated check-out eliminates standing shared passwords and provides a complete audit trail of privileged access.

sub_capabilities.name: Session Recording / Monitoring
sub_capabilities.rationale: Video and keystroke recording of privileged sessions enables forensic investigation and deters malicious insider activity.

sub_capabilities.name: Just-In-Time Access
sub_capabilities.rationale: Eliminating standing privileged access and granting time-limited elevation on request reduces the window of exposure from compromised credentials.

sub_capabilities.name: Automated Password Rotation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Rotating privileged credentials on a schedule or after each use ensures compromised credentials have minimal useful lifetime.

sub_capabilities.name: MFA on Privileged Escalation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Requiring re-authentication with MFA at the point of privilege escalation adds a critical step-up control that thwarts lateral movement.

capabilities.name: IGA
capabilities.catalog_intro: The authoritative record of who has access to what and why. IGA closes the joiner/mover/leaver gap and enables access certification at audit time.
capabilities.description: Identity governance, provisioning, access certification

sub_capabilities.name: Access Certification / Reviews
sub_capabilities.rationale: Periodic manager-led certification campaigns identify and revoke accumulated entitlements, satisfying SOX, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 audit requirements.

sub_capabilities.name: Automated Provisioning / Deprovisioning
sub_capabilities.rationale: Policy-driven automated provisioning and deprovisioning eliminates the manual process lag that leaves orphaned accounts exposed.

sub_capabilities.name: Role / Entitlement Management
sub_capabilities.rationale: Role-based access models map entitlements to business functions, making access requests, approvals, and reviews understandable to non-technical reviewers.

sub_capabilities.name: Segregation of Duties (SoD)
sub_capabilities.rationale: Automated SoD conflict detection prevents users from accumulating entitlements that, in combination, would enable fraud or compliance violations.

sub_capabilities.name: Audit Trail / Compliance Reporting
sub_capabilities.rationale: Immutable audit logs of all provisioning events and certification decisions provide the evidence trail required by external auditors.

capabilities.name: SASE / ZTNA
capabilities.catalog_intro: Zero-trust access for the distributed workforce. Replaces perimeter VPN with identity-aware, application-level access controls delivered from the cloud edge.
capabilities.description: Zero-trust network access, cloud-delivered security service edge

sub_capabilities.name: Zero-Trust Network Access
sub_capabilities.rationale: Identity and device-aware application access eliminates implicit network trust granted by legacy VPN, reducing lateral movement risk.

sub_capabilities.name: Secure Web Gateway
sub_capabilities.rationale: Cloud-delivered web proxy inspects all user internet traffic regardless of device location, closing the inspection gap created by remote work.

sub_capabilities.name: CASB
sub_capabilities.rationale: Cloud access security brokering provides visibility and control over sanctioned and unsanctioned SaaS usage, data sharing, and OAuth grants.

sub_capabilities.name: FWaaS
sub_capabilities.rationale: Cloud-delivered firewall capability extends NGFW-class inspection to branch and remote users without backhauling traffic to a central data centre.

sub_capabilities.name: DLP / Data Inspection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Inline data loss prevention on cloud-routed traffic enforces data handling policies for remote users without requiring endpoint agent coverage.

sub_capabilities.name: SD-WAN
sub_capabilities.rationale: Software-defined WAN integrated with SSE provides optimised, policy-aware routing for branch office traffic alongside security inspection.

capabilities.name: Data Security / DLP
capabilities.catalog_intro: Know where your sensitive data lives and ensure it doesn't leave without authorisation. DSPM and DLP together address both discovery and enforcement.
capabilities.description: Data loss prevention, DSPM, classification, insider threat

sub_capabilities.name: Data Discovery / Classification
sub_capabilities.rationale: Automated discovery and classification of sensitive data across structured and unstructured stores is a prerequisite for any meaningful data protection programme.

sub_capabilities.name: DLP Policy Enforcement
sub_capabilities.rationale: Content-aware policy enforcement on email, endpoint, and cloud channels prevents sensitive data from leaving under both accidental and malicious conditions.

sub_capabilities.name: Encryption at Rest / In Transit
sub_capabilities.rationale: Encryption of sensitive data at rest and in transit ensures that physical theft or interception does not result in data exposure.

sub_capabilities.name: DSPM / Cloud Data Posture
sub_capabilities.rationale: Data security posture management extends classification and risk visibility to cloud object stores and databases where traditional DLP has no reach.

sub_capabilities.name: Insider Threat Detection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Behavioural analytics layered over DLP telemetry identifies unusual data access and exfiltration patterns that policy rules alone cannot catch.

capabilities.name: OT / ICS Security
capabilities.catalog_intro: Industrial and operational technology environments have different risk profiles — safety, availability, and legacy protocol constraints demand specialised tooling.
capabilities.description: Operational technology, industrial control system monitoring

sub_capabilities.name: OT Asset Visibility
sub_capabilities.rationale: Passive discovery of OT assets — PLCs, HMIs, RTUs — without disrupting live processes is the first step in any ICS security programme.

sub_capabilities.name: Network Anomaly Detection
sub_capabilities.rationale: Baselining OT network behaviour and alerting on deviations identifies lateral movement and unauthorised command execution without active scanning.

sub_capabilities.name: Industrial Protocol Monitoring
sub_capabilities.rationale: Deep packet inspection of Modbus, DNP3, EtherNet/IP, and similar protocols provides command-level visibility that generic network tools lack.

sub_capabilities.name: IT/OT Segmentation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Enforced network boundaries between IT and OT zones limit the blast radius of an IT-side breach reaching process control networks.

sub_capabilities.name: OT Incident Response
sub_capabilities.rationale: OT-aware IR playbooks that account for safety system impacts and operational continuity requirements are materially different from IT IR procedures.

capabilities.name: DevOps / AppSec
capabilities.catalog_intro: Shift security left into the development pipeline. Finding vulnerabilities in code and dependencies before deployment is orders of magnitude cheaper than post-release.
capabilities.description: SAST, DAST, SCA, secrets scanning, pipeline security

sub_capabilities.name: SAST / Code Analysis
sub_capabilities.rationale: Static analysis of first-party code during development identifies injection flaws, logic errors, and insecure patterns before they reach production.

sub_capabilities.name: SCA / Dependency Scanning
sub_capabilities.rationale: Software composition analysis identifies vulnerable third-party libraries and licence risks across the full dependency tree.

sub_capabilities.name: Secrets Scanning
sub_capabilities.rationale: Automated detection of hardcoded API keys, tokens, and certificates in source code and git history prevents trivial credential compromise.

sub_capabilities.name: DAST / API Testing
sub_capabilities.rationale: Dynamic testing against running applications finds runtime vulnerabilities — authentication issues, business logic flaws — that SAST cannot detect statically.

sub_capabilities.name: Pipeline / CI Security
sub_capabilities.rationale: Hardening CI/CD pipelines — build environment isolation, signed artefacts, approval gates — prevents supply chain attacks that compromise the delivery mechanism itself.

capabilities.name: Non-Human Identity
capabilities.catalog_intro: Service accounts, API keys, machine credentials, and certificates now outnumber human identities 10-to-1. This is the fastest-growing attack surface in modern enterprise environments.
capabilities.description: Machine identity, secrets, certificates

sub_capabilities.name: Service Account Governance
sub_capabilities.rationale: Unmanaged service accounts are persistent attacker footholds; governance enforces least-privilege and rotation.

sub_capabilities.name: API Key & Secret Rotation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Leaked API keys are a leading cloud breach vector; automated rotation eliminates long-lived credential risk.

sub_capabilities.name: Certificate Lifecycle Management
sub_capabilities.rationale: Expired certificates cause outages and leave services vulnerable; automated lifecycle management eliminates manual expiry risk.

sub_capabilities.name: Workload Identity Federation
sub_capabilities.rationale: SPIFFE/SPIRE and cloud workload identity allows services to authenticate without static credentials, eliminating the secrets sprawl problem.

sub_capabilities.name: Secrets Injection & Vaulting
sub_capabilities.rationale: Runtime secrets injection via agent or sidecar ensures applications never store secrets at rest in code or config files.

capabilities.name: Governance, Risk & Compliance
capabilities.catalog_intro: The control framework for your security program. GRC platforms convert point-in-time audits into continuous assurance — essential for SOC 2, ISO 27001, MAS TRM, and regulatory obligations.
capabilities.description: Policy, risk register, compliance automation

sub_capabilities.name: Policy Management & Attestation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Documented, attested policies are the contractual foundation of any compliance program and the first ask of every auditor.

sub_capabilities.name: Compliance Automation
sub_capabilities.rationale: Continuous automated evidence collection reduces audit prep from months to days and eliminates point-in-time compliance theatre.

sub_capabilities.name: Risk Register & Treatment
sub_capabilities.rationale: A quantified risk register enables business-language risk decisions and demonstrates to regulators that risk is managed, not ignored.

sub_capabilities.name: Audit Management
sub_capabilities.rationale: Structured audit workflows, finding tracking, and remediation evidence chains reduce audit cycle time and repeat findings.

capabilities.name: Third-Party Risk Management
capabilities.catalog_intro: Over 60% of breaches have a third-party component. TPRM converts vendor security from a checkbox questionnaire into a continuous, risk-scored programme.
capabilities.description: Vendor risk scoring, questionnaires, supply chain

sub_capabilities.name: Vendor Risk Questionnaire
sub_capabilities.rationale: Standardised security questionnaires (CAIQ, SIG, custom) establish a baseline risk picture for every critical supplier.

sub_capabilities.name: Continuous Vendor Monitoring
sub_capabilities.rationale: Point-in-time questionnaires miss the 90% of the year between assessments; continuous external scoring detects deterioration in real time.

sub_capabilities.name: Contract & DPA Risk
sub_capabilities.rationale: Data processing agreements and security annexes create contractual obligations that align vendor incentives with your security posture.

sub_capabilities.name: Fourth-Party / Sub-processor Risk
sub_capabilities.rationale: Supply chain attacks increasingly exploit sub-processors; mapping n-tier vendor relationships identifies concentration risk before it bites.

capabilities.name: Post-Quantum Crypto
capabilities.catalog_intro: The harvest-now-decrypt-later threat is already active. NIST PQC standards (FIPS 203/204/205) are finalised. Enterprises with long data-classification windows need to start migration planning now.
capabilities.description: PQC key management, HSM, algorithm migration

sub_capabilities.name: PQC Key Management
sub_capabilities.rationale: HSMs and KMS must support ML-KEM/ML-DSA to protect long-lived secrets against future quantum decryption.

sub_capabilities.name: PQC-Ready TLS
sub_capabilities.rationale: Hybrid TLS (classical + PQC) protects data in transit against harvest-now attacks during the migration window.

sub_capabilities.name: Crypto-Agility Assessment
sub_capabilities.rationale: Inventory classical algorithms in use (RSA, ECC) and prioritise migration order based on data classification and key lifetime.

capabilities.name: ITSM / CMDB
capabilities.catalog_intro: The connective tissue of security operations. Without an authoritative CMDB, vulnerability findings have no owner, security tickets cannot route correctly, and incident response lacks the asset context needed to scope blast radius. ITSM workflows close the loop between detection and remediation — without them, patching SLAs exist only on paper. For APAC financial institutions under MAS TRM §4.3 and HKMA operational resilience guidance, a well-maintained CMDB is an explicit audit requirement.
capabilities.description: IT service management, change control, and authoritative asset inventory

capabilities.name: Threat Intelligence
capabilities.catalog_intro: The difference between reacting to yesterday's attack and preparing for tomorrow's. A standalone TIP aggregates finished intelligence from commercial, government, and open-source feeds, maps actor TTPs to your environment using MITRE ATT&CK, and produces actionable briefings for both the SOC and the board. For APAC financial institutions, dedicated TI infrastructure is the primary tool for tracking Lazarus Group, APT41, and regional criminal syndicates. IOC enrichment inside your SIEM or EDR is not a substitute — it lacks the actor-tracking and strategic analysis layer that separates intelligence from data.
capabilities.description: Strategic and operational threat intelligence aggregation, actor tracking, and TTP analysis

capabilities.name: Observability / APM
capabilities.catalog_intro: Operational resilience is inseparable from security resilience. When a DDoS attack hits or ransomware begins encrypting shared drives, the first signal is almost always a performance anomaly — not a security alert. Application performance monitoring and distributed tracing give the SOC early warning of service degradation, help distinguish security events from operational failures, and provide the response metrics that HKMA and MAS now require from regulated institutions. CISOs who do not own this tooling still answer for it when regulators ask about operational resilience.
capabilities.description: Application performance monitoring, distributed tracing, and infrastructure telemetry

capabilities.name: Digital Risk Protection
capabilities.catalog_intro: The attacker doesn't need to breach your perimeter if they can impersonate your brand convincingly enough. Digital risk protection monitors the external threat surface — fake domains, spoofed mobile apps in Asian app stores, phishing kits mimicking your internet banking portal, executive impersonation on LinkedIn, and credential dumps on dark web forums. For APAC financial institutions, MAS TRM §6.3 requires monitoring for technology risk events that originate outside the institution's own systems.
capabilities.description: External brand monitoring, dark web intelligence, and phishing/fraud takedown
